requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must use portion of home regularly and exclusively for business as principal place of business or for meeting clients, patients, or customers to deduct allocable home expenses
criteria: Must use specific area of home exclusively and regularly for business purposes (strict exclusive use test)
Principal place of business - where administrative or management activities conducted and no other fixed location for these activities
Separate structure exception allows deduction for detached garage, studio, or barn used in connection with business
Place to meet patients, clients, or customers in normal course of business (even if not principal place)
Daycare facility exception allows personal use but must meet specific licensing and regulatory requirements
Choose between simplified method ($5/sq ft up to 300 sq ft = $1,500 max) or actual expense method
Actual method requires allocation of expenses based on percentage of home used for business
Deduction limited to gross income from business use of home less business expenses (no loss creation)

home_office_requirements.exclusive_use_test: Area must be used exclusively for business; mixed personal/business use disqualifies entire space
home_office_requirements.regular_use_test: Must use space on continuing, ongoing basis; occasional or incidental use insufficient
home_office_requirements.principal_place_test: Either conduct administrative/management activities there or meet clients/customers regularly
home_office_requirements.simplified_method: $5 per square foot for up to 300 square feet (maximum $1,500 deduction annually)
home_office_requirements.actual_expense_method: Deduct allocable portion of mortgage interest, property taxes, utilities, repairs, depreciation, insurance
home_office_requirements.employee_restrictions: Employees must use home office for employer's convenience, not merely appropriate or helpful

restrictions: W-2 employees generally cannot claim home office deduction (suspended through 2025 as miscellaneous itemized deduction)
Deduction cannot exceed gross income from business use after deducting business portion of mortgage interest and property taxes
Excess deduction carries forward to future years subject to same income limitation
Depreciation recapture applies when selling home if claimed home office depreciation under actual expense method
Simplified method does not allow separate depreciation deduction and does not reduce home sale exclusion basis
Exclusive use test strictly enforced; personal use of space (even minimal) disqualifies entire deduction
Daycare exception requires state licensing, regular business use, and careful tracking of business vs personal hours
Allocation methods (square footage most common) must be reasonable and consistent year-over-year
